sections.paragraph:
(from the root הָמָה), m. (f. in one passage, Job 31:34).

(1) the sound, noise of a shower, 1Ki 18:41 of singers, Eze 26:13 Amo 5:23 especially of a multitude, 1Sa 4:14 14:19 Job 39:7. Hence-

(2) a multitude of men itself. קֹול הָמֹון the noise of a multitude, Isa 13:4 33:3 Dan 10:6. הֲמֹון גֹּויִם Gen 17:4, Gen 17:5. ה׳ עַמִּים Isa 17:12, many peoples. הֲמֹון נָשִׁים a multitude of women, 2Ch 11:23. Especially used of hosts of soldiers, Jud. 4:7 Dan 11:11, 12 Dan 11:12, 13 Dan 11:13. Also, plenty of water, Jer 10:13 51:16 .

(3) plenty, wealth, riches, Psa 37:16 Ecc 5:9 Isa 60:5.

(4) emotion of mind. Isa 63:15, הֲמֹון מֵעֶיךָ pr. “commotion of thy bowels,” i.e. thy mercy; comp. the root No. 3.
